Are barcode scanning requirements configured?
common_mistakes: Making all products available in POS when only a subset should be.
POS-DREQ-002.odoo_docs_hint: Point of Sale > Configuration > Point of Sale > [POS] > Products

POS-DREQ-003.title: POS cashier access control
POS-DREQ-003.what: Informational checkpoint confirming cashier access roles are configured. Depends on USR-DREQ-001 (role baseline).
POS-DREQ-003.why: Cashier access determines who can operate POS sessions, apply discounts, and process refunds. Without role controls, any user can perform any POS action.
confirm_first: Which users are authorized to operate POS sessions?
Are there restrictions on discount or refund authority for cashiers?
Is PIN-based cashier login required?
common_mistakes: Not restricting cashier roles, allowing any employee to process sales and refunds.
POS-DREQ-003.odoo_docs_hint: Point of Sale > Configuration > Point of Sale > [POS] > Employees

POS-DREQ-004.title: POS-accounting journal linkage
POS-DREQ-004.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-01=Full accounting). Informational confirmation that POS journals are linked to the correct accounting journals.
POS-DREQ-004.why: POS transactions generate journal entries. If journals are not linked, POS revenue and cash movements do not appear in accounting.
confirm_first: Is the POS sales journal linked to the correct revenue accounts?
Are cash and card payment methods linked to the correct bank/cash journals?
Has a test POS session been reconciled against accounting?
common_mistakes: Not testing the POS-to-accounting flow before go-live.
POS-DREQ-004.odoo_docs_hint: Point of Sale > Configuration > Payment Methods > [Method] > Journal

POS-DREQ-005.title: POS-inventory stock linkage
POS-DREQ-005.what: Conditional checkpoint (OP-01=Yes). Informational confirmation that POS is configured to decrement stock on sales.
POS-DREQ-005.why: Without stock linkage, POS sales do not reduce inventory quantities, making stock levels unreliable.
confirm_first: Should POS sales decrement inventory in real-time?
Is the POS linked to the correct warehouse location?
Have lot/serial tracking requirements been considered for POS products?
common_mistakes: Forgetting to link POS to a warehouse, causing POS sales to not affect inventory.
POS-DREQ-005.odoo_docs_hint: Point of Sale > Configuration > Point of Sale > [POS] > Inventory

POS-GL-001.title: POS go-live readiness
POS-GL-001.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming POS configuration is finalized for store operations.
POS-GL-001.why: POS errors after go-live directly affect customers and cash management. All configuration must be tested before stores open.
confirm_first: Has a complete POS session been tested (open, sell, close, reconcile)?
Are all payment methods working (cash, card, etc.)?
Has the store team been trained on the POS interface?
common_mistakes: Going live with POS without testing the full session lifecycle including closing and reconciliation.

website_ecommerce.label: Website / eCommerce
website_ecommerce.overview: Governs customer-facing digital commerce, catalog publication, checkout, and order integration. Coverage gap: website and website_sale models not in governed apply surface — assembler returns empty map.

WEB-FOUND-001.title: Website scope baseline
WEB-FOUND-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming website module is activated and baseline configuration is reviewed. No governed write — assembler returns empty map.
WEB-FOUND-001.why: Website decisions affect customer experience, SEO, and order flow. Without a baseline, downstream website configuration lacks context.
confirm_first: Is the website module installed?
Has the website domain and company been configured?
What is the primary purpose of the website (eCommerce, catalog, corporate)?
common_mistakes: Publishing the website before content and checkout configuration are complete.

WEB-DREQ-001.title: Catalog and product publication baseline
WEB-DREQ-001.what: Configures which products are published on the website and how they appear to customers.
WEB-DREQ-001.why: Published products are visible to anyone. Without publication governance, internal or draft products may appear on the public website.
confirm_first: Which products should be published on the website?
Are product images and descriptions ready for customer viewing?
Has the product categorization for website navigation been configured?
common_mistakes: Publishing all products by default, including internal-use or draft items.

WEB-DREQ-002.title: Payment provider configuration
WEB-DREQ-002.what: Informational checkpoint confirming payment providers are configured for online checkout. No governed write — coverage gap.
WEB-DREQ-002.why: Without payment providers, customers cannot complete purchases on the website. Misconfigured providers cause payment failures.
confirm_first: Which payment providers will be enabled (Stripe, PayPal, etc.)?
Have payment provider credentials been configured and tested?
Is the payment flow tested in a sandbox environment?
common_mistakes: Going live with production payment credentials without testing in sandbox mode first.

WEB-DREQ-003.title: Customer account and access configuration
WEB-DREQ-003.what: Configures customer portal access rules — whether customers can browse as guests, must register, or use B2B portal features.
WEB-DREQ-003.why: Customer access rules affect conversion rates and data capture. Too restrictive = lost sales; too open = no customer data for CRM.
confirm_first: Can customers browse and purchase as guests?
Is customer registration required before checkout?
Are there B2B customers who need portal access to order history and invoices?
common_mistakes: Requiring registration for all customers, creating friction that reduces conversions.

WEB-DREQ-004.title: Website-inventory delivery integration
WEB-DREQ-004.what: Conditional checkpoint (OP-01=Yes). Configures delivery methods and shipping cost computation for eCommerce orders.
WEB-DREQ-004.why: Delivery methods determine shipping options and costs at checkout. Without configuration, customers cannot select delivery or see shipping costs.
confirm_first: Which delivery methods are available for online orders?
Are shipping rates computed by carrier integration or flat rate?
Has a test order been placed and fulfilled end-to-end?
common_mistakes: Not configuring delivery methods, causing checkout to fail at the shipping step.

WEB-DREQ-005.title: Website-sales quotation template integration
WEB-DREQ-005.what: Conditional checkpoint (SC-03=Yes). Configures quotation templates for online sales proposals and B2B portal quotes.
WEB-DREQ-005.why: Quotation templates create consistent online sales proposals. Without integration, online quotes may differ from sales team quotes.
confirm_first: Will online quotations use the same templates as direct sales?
Are optional products configured on website quotation templates?
Has the online quotation signing flow been tested?
common_mistakes: Having different quotation templates online vs. offline, creating customer confusion.

WEB-GL-001.title: Website/eCommerce go-live readiness
WEB-GL-001.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming the website and eCommerce configuration is finalized for public launch.
WEB-GL-001.why: Website launch exposes the business to customers. Errors in checkout, pricing, or delivery create immediate revenue and reputation impact.
confirm_first: Has the full purchase flow been tested (browse, cart, checkout, payment, delivery)?
Are all product prices, descriptions, and images correct?
Has the website been tested on mobile devices?
common_mistakes: Launching the website without testing the complete customer journey end-to-end.

projects.label: Projects
projects.overview: Governs project delivery structures, task flow, billing linkage, and ownership. Coverage gap: project.project and project.task.type are not in the governed apply surface.

PRJ-FOUND-001.title: Project structure foundation
PRJ-FOUND-001.what: Establishes the project structure baseline — project types, task stages, and organizational context. Coverage gap: project.project not in governed apply surface.
PRJ-FOUND-001.why: Project structure determines how work is organized, tracked, and billed. Without a baseline, projects are created ad-hoc without consistent governance.
confirm_first: What types of projects does the business run (internal, client-facing, fixed-price, T&M)?
Has a task stage workflow been defined?
Who are the project managers?
common_mistakes: Creating projects without defining task stages, losing visibility into project progress.

PRJ-DREQ-001.title: Task flow and stage configuration
PRJ-DREQ-001.what: Configures task stages and workflow rules. Coverage gap: project.task.type not in governed apply surface.
PRJ-DREQ-001.why: Task stages drive Kanban views, automation rules, and project reporting. Without defined stages, project tracking is unstructured.
confirm_first: What are the task stages for each project type?
Are there automation rules for stage transitions?
Has the Kanban view been configured for the project team's workflow?
common_mistakes: Using default stages that do not match the client's project management methodology.

PRJ-DREQ-002.title: Project billing linkage
PRJ-DREQ-002.what: Configures the linkage between projects and sales/invoicing. Depends on SAL-DREQ-001 (sales baseline).
PRJ-DREQ-002.why: Project billing determines how time and materials or fixed-price projects generate invoices. Without linkage, billable work is not invoiced.
confirm_first: Are projects billed as time & materials or fixed price?
Has the service product been configured with the correct invoicing policy?
Has a test project been billed to verify the invoice flow?
common_mistakes: Configuring project billing without testing that timesheets correctly feed into invoices.

PRJ-DREQ-003.title: Project timesheet integration
PRJ-DREQ-003.what: Conditional checkpoint (RM-02=Yes). Configures timesheet integration with projects for time tracking and billing.
PRJ-DREQ-003.why: Timesheets provide the data for T&M billing and project profitability analysis. Without integration, time data cannot be converted to invoices.
confirm_first: Which projects require timesheet tracking?
Will timesheets be used for billing, tracking, or both?
Has the timesheet approval workflow been defined?
common_mistakes: Enabling timesheets without configuring the hourly rate on the service product.

PRJ-DREQ-004.title: Project analytic accounting linkage
PRJ-DREQ-004.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-05=Yes). Configures analytic accounting linkage for project cost tracking. Depends on ACCT-REC-001.
PRJ-DREQ-004.why: Analytic accounts enable project-level profitability analysis. Without linkage, project costs cannot be tracked against revenue.
confirm_first: Should each project automatically create an analytic account?
Has the analytic plan been configured for project costing?
Will analytic accounts be used for both revenue and cost tracking?
common_mistakes: Not enabling automatic analytic account creation on projects, requiring manual setup for each project.

PRJ-GL-001.title: Projects go-live readiness
PRJ-GL-001.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming project configuration is finalized for operational use.
PRJ-GL-001.why: Project configuration errors after go-live affect billing accuracy, time tracking, and resource visibility.
confirm_first: Has a complete project lifecycle been tested (create, tasks, timesheets, billing)?
Are all task stages and project templates finalized?
Has the project team been trained?
common_mistakes: Going live with projects without testing the billing-to-invoice flow.

quality.label: Quality
quality.overview: Governs quality control points, checks, and inspection flow on quality.point. Quality depends on inventory operation types being configured. Coverage gap: quality.alert not in governed apply surface.

QUA-FOUND-001.title: Quality control baseline
QUA-FOUND-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming quality module is activated and baseline quality control strategy is reviewed. No governed write.
QUA-FOUND-001.why: Quality control must be designed before quality points are created. Without a strategy, quality checks are either missing or create unnecessary bottlenecks.
confirm_first: Is the Quality module installed?
At which operations should quality checks occur (receipt, production, delivery)?
What type of quality checks are needed (pass/fail, measure, picture)?
common_mistakes: Adding quality checks on every operation, creating warehouse bottlenecks.

QUA-DREQ-001.title: Receipt quality control points
QUA-DREQ-001.what: Conditional checkpoint (MF-06 includes Receipt). Informational confirmation that quality control points are configured for receiving operations.
QUA-DREQ-001.why: Receipt quality checks ensure incoming materials meet standards before entering inventory. Without them, defective materials contaminate stock.
confirm_first: Which products require receipt quality inspection?
What are the pass/fail criteria?
What happens when a receipt fails quality (quarantine, return)?
common_mistakes: Requiring quality checks on all receipts when only critical materials need inspection.
QUA-DREQ-001.odoo_docs_hint: Quality > Configuration > Quality Control Points > [Point] > Operation: Receipts

QUA-DREQ-002.title: In-process quality control points
QUA-DREQ-002.what: Conditional checkpoint (MF-06 includes In-process). Informational confirmation that quality control points are configured for manufacturing operations. Depends on MRP-DREQ-005.
QUA-DREQ-002.why: In-process quality checks catch defects during production before more value is added. Without them, defects are only found at finished goods inspection.
confirm_first: At which work center operations should quality checks occur?
Are there specific measurements or tolerances to check?
What is the rework or scrap process for failed checks?
common_mistakes: Not integrating quality checks into routing steps, making them manual and easily skipped.
QUA-DREQ-002.odoo_docs_hint: Quality > Configuration > Quality Control Points > [Point] > Operation: Manufacturing

QUA-DREQ-003.title: Finished goods quality control points
QUA-DREQ-003.what: Conditional checkpoint (MF-06 includes Finished goods). Informational confirmation that quality control points are configured for finished goods inspection.
QUA-DREQ-003.why: Finished goods inspection is the last check before products reach customers. Skipping it risks shipping defective products.
confirm_first: Which finished goods require final inspection?
What are the acceptance criteria?
How are failed finished goods handled (rework, scrap, quarantine)?
common_mistakes: Not configuring a quarantine location for failed finished goods.
QUA-DREQ-003.odoo_docs_hint: Quality > Configuration > Quality Control Points > [Point] > Operation: Delivery Orders

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.overview: Governs equipment maintenance planning and service execution. Maintenance depends on manufacturing work centers being configured.

MNT-FOUND-001.title: Maintenance module foundation
MNT-FOUND-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming maintenance module is activated and equipment register baseline is reviewed. Depends on MRP-DREQ-005 (work centers).
MNT-FOUND-001.why: Maintenance planning requires an equipment register. Without it, preventive maintenance cannot be scheduled and reactive maintenance is untracked.
confirm_first: Is the Maintenance module installed?
Has the equipment register been populated?
Are equipment categories defined?
common_mistakes: Installing maintenance without first registering equipment.

MNT-DREQ-001.title: Preventive maintenance schedule
MNT-DREQ-001.what: Configures preventive maintenance schedules — frequency, responsible teams, and maintenance types.
MNT-DREQ-001.why: Preventive maintenance reduces unplanned downtime. Without schedules, maintenance is reactive only, increasing costs and production disruption.
confirm_first: Which equipment requires preventive maintenance?
What are the maintenance frequencies (daily, weekly, monthly)?
Who is responsible for each type of maintenance?
common_mistakes: Creating maintenance schedules without assigning responsible teams.

MNT-DREQ-002.title: Maintenance team configuration
MNT-DREQ-002.what: Configures maintenance teams and assigns team members. Depends on USR-DREQ-001 (role baseline).
MNT-DREQ-002.why: Maintenance requests are routed to teams. Without team configuration, maintenance requests have no assignee and remain unresolved.
confirm_first: How many maintenance teams are needed?
Who are the team members and team leaders?
Are teams specialized (electrical, mechanical) or generalist?
common_mistakes: Creating only one maintenance team when specialized teams are needed for proper routing.

MNT-REC-001.title: Maintenance-equipment linkage readiness
MNT-REC-001.what: Recommended checkpoint confirming equipment records are linked to work centers, locations, and maintenance schedules.
MNT-REC-001.why: Equipment linked to work centers enables maintenance impact analysis — if a work center is down for maintenance, production scheduling adjusts.
confirm_first: Are equipment records linked to the correct work centers?
Is the equipment location set correctly?
Has the maintenance history import been considered?
common_mistakes: Not linking equipment to work centers, losing the connection between maintenance and production.

repairs.label: Repairs
repairs.overview: Governs controlled repair workflows for repair operations. Repairs depends on inventory and master data for parts tracking and billing. Coverage gap: repair.order not in governed apply surface.

REP-FOUND-001.title: Repairs module foundation
REP-FOUND-001.what: Informational checkpoint confirming the repair module is activated and baseline repair workflow is reviewed. Depends on INV-FOUND-003.
REP-FOUND-001.why: Repair operations consume parts from inventory and may generate invoices. Without foundation, repairs are untracked and parts consumption is unmanaged.
confirm_first: Is the Repairs module installed?
What types of repairs does the business perform?
Are repair operations linked to specific product categories?
common_mistakes: Activating repairs without inventory foundation, causing parts consumption failures.

REP-DREQ-001.title: Repair order workflow and parts management
REP-DREQ-001.what: Configures repair order workflow including parts consumption, labor tracking, and repair status flow. Depends on MAS-DREQ-001.
REP-DREQ-001.why: Repair orders consume inventory parts. Without proper configuration, parts are consumed without tracking or cost allocation.
confirm_first: Are repair parts stocked and tracked in inventory?
Is labor time tracked on repair orders?
Has the repair status workflow been defined (draft, confirmed, in progress, done)?
common_mistakes: Not configuring parts consumption routes, causing repairs to not affect inventory.

REP-DREQ-002.title: Repair billing configuration
REP-DREQ-002.what: Configures repair billing — whether repairs are billable to customers, warranty-covered, or internal. Depends on SAL-DREQ-001.
REP-DREQ-002.why: Repair billing determines whether costs are recovered from customers or absorbed internally. Without configuration, billable repairs may not generate invoices.
confirm_first: Are repairs billable to external customers?
Is there a warranty policy that affects billing?
Has the repair invoicing flow been tested?
common_mistakes: Not distinguishing between warranty repairs (free) and billable repairs.

REP-REC-001.title: Repair reporting readiness
REP-REC-001.what: Recommended checkpoint confirming repair reporting and analytics are configured for operational insights.
REP-REC-001.why: Repair data reveals product quality issues, warranty costs, and customer satisfaction trends. Without reporting, this data is not actionable.
confirm_first: What repair metrics does management want to track?
Are repair categories configured for meaningful reporting?
Has the repair reporting dashboard been reviewed?
common_mistakes: Not configuring repair categories, making it impossible to analyze repair trends by type.

documents.label: Documents
documents.overview: Governs structured document storage, retrieval, and operational document workflows. Documents domain has Safe write class. Coverage gap: documents.document not in governed apply surface.

DOC-FOUND-001.title: Document workspace foundation
DOC-FOUND-001.what: Establishes document workspace structure — workspaces, folder hierarchy, and base access rules.
DOC-FOUND-001.why: Workspaces organize documents by business function. Without structure, documents are stored in a flat list with no governance.
confirm_first: What workspace categories does the business need (HR, Contracts, Projects)?
Who should have access to each workspace?
Will documents be linked to operational records (invoices, purchase orders)?
common_mistakes: Creating too many workspaces without a clear naming convention.

DOC-DREQ-001.title: Document access control and workflow rules
DOC-DREQ-001.what: Configures document access control and workflow automation rules. Depends on USR-DREQ-002 (access rights configuration).
DOC-DREQ-001.why: Document access rules protect sensitive information. Without them, all users can access all documents regardless of sensitivity.
confirm_first: Are there documents with restricted access requirements (contracts, HR records)?
Are workflow automation rules needed (e.g., auto-tag invoices)?
Has the access policy been reviewed with the document owner?
common_mistakes: Leaving all documents accessible to all internal users.

DOC-REC-001.title: Document operational linkage
DOC-REC-001.what: Recommended checkpoint confirming documents are linked to operational records for traceability.
DOC-REC-001.why: Linking documents to records (invoices, orders, contracts) provides audit trail and reduces time spent searching for supporting documentation.
confirm_first: Which record types should have document linkage?
Are document tags configured for search and filtering?
Has the document upload process been tested?
common_mistakes: Not configuring automatic document actions, missing the automation benefits of the Documents module.

sign.label: Sign
sign.overview: Governs signature workflows, signer roles, and document approval execution. Coverage gap: sign.template not in governed apply surface.

SGN-FOUND-001.title: Signature template foundation
SGN-FOUND-001.what: Establishes the signature template baseline — template types, field placement, and foundational signing workflow.
SGN-FOUND-001.why: Signature templates define how documents are presented for signing. Without templates, every signature request must be configured from scratch.
confirm_first: What types of documents will be sent for signature (contracts, NDAs, approvals)?
Has the template library been designed?
Are signature field placements (name, date, signature) defined?
common_mistakes: Creating templates without testing the signing experience from the signer's perspective.

SGN-DREQ-001.title: Signer role and access configuration
SGN-DREQ-001.what: Configures who can send, sign, and manage signature requests. Depends on USR-DREQ-001 (role baseline).
SGN-DREQ-001.why: Signer roles control who can initiate and complete signature workflows. Without configuration, signature requests may not reach the right people.
confirm_first: Who is authorized to send signature requests?
Are there multi-signer templates that require specific role ordering?
Has the signature request-to-completion flow been tested?
common_mistakes: Not testing the email notification flow for external signers.

SGN-DREQ-002.title: Signature traceability and record linkage
SGN-DREQ-002.what: Configures how signed documents are stored, linked to records, and made available for audit.
SGN-DREQ-002.why: Signed documents have legal significance. Without traceability, signed documents may be lost or disconnected from the records they support.
confirm_first: Where will signed documents be stored (Documents module, attached to records)?
Is there a retention policy for signed documents?
Has the audit trail for completed signatures been reviewed?
common_mistakes: Not linking signed documents to the originating record (contract, agreement).

approvals.label: Approvals
approvals.overview: Governs formal approval requests outside individual module approval rules. Depends on Users/Roles approval design. Coverage gap: approval.category not in governed apply surface.

APR-FOUND-001.title: Approval structure foundation
APR-FOUND-001.what: Establishes the approval module baseline — approval categories, minimum approvers, and general approval workflow. Depends on USR-DREQ-003 (privileged access review).
APR-FOUND-001.why: The Approvals module centralizes requests that do not fit into module-specific approval workflows (e.g., budget requests, special expenditures).
confirm_first: What types of approval requests does the business need beyond module-specific approvals?
Who are the approvers for each category?
What is the minimum number of approvals required?
common_mistakes: Creating approval categories that duplicate module-specific approvals (e.g., purchase approval).

APR-DREQ-001.title: Approval category and approver configuration
APR-DREQ-001.what: Configures specific approval categories and assigns approvers for each category.
APR-DREQ-001.why: Each approval category needs designated approvers. Without them, approval requests cannot be processed.
confirm_first: Has each approval category been assigned at least one approver?
Are there backup approvers for each category?
Has the approval notification flow been tested?
common_mistakes: Creating approval categories without assigning approvers.

APR-DREQ-002.title: Approval authority and traceability
APR-DREQ-002.what: Configures approval authority controls and audit traceability for approval decisions.
APR-DREQ-002.why: Approval decisions must be traceable for compliance. Without authority controls, anyone could approve any request.
confirm_first: Are approval authority levels defined (e.g., department manager, director, VP)?
Is there an escalation path for delayed approvals?
Has the approval audit trail been verified?
common_mistakes: Not configuring escalation, causing approval requests to stall indefinitely.

APR-DREQ-003.title: Inventory adjustment approval
APR-DREQ-003.what: Conditional checkpoint (TA-03 includes Inventory adjustments). Configures approval requirements for inventory adjustment requests.
APR-DREQ-003.why: Inventory adjustments directly affect stock values and accounting. Without approval controls, unauthorized adjustments can hide shrinkage or errors.
confirm_first: Who can request inventory adjustments?
Who must approve them?
Is there a threshold below which adjustments do not require approval?
common_mistakes: Allowing inventory adjustments without any approval requirement.

APR-DREQ-004.title: Expense approval configuration
APR-DREQ-004.what: Conditional checkpoint (TA-03 includes Expenses). Configures approval requirements for expense reports.
APR-DREQ-004.why: Expense approvals are a fundamental financial control. Without them, expenses are reimbursed without review.
confirm_first: Who approves expense reports (direct manager, finance)?
Are there expense thresholds that require additional approval?
Has the expense-to-reimbursement flow been tested?
common_mistakes: Not defining expense categories, making expense analysis impossible.

APR-DREQ-005.title: Manufacturing order approval
APR-DREQ-005.what: Conditional checkpoint (TA-03 includes Manufacturing order). Configures approval requirements for manufacturing orders.
APR-DREQ-005.why: Manufacturing order approval ensures production capacity and material availability are confirmed before committing to production.
confirm_first: Do all manufacturing orders require approval or only above certain quantities?
Who approves manufacturing orders?
Has the MO approval workflow been tested?
common_mistakes: Requiring approval on all MOs when only large or non-standard orders need oversight.

APR-DREQ-006.title: HR leave approval configuration
APR-DREQ-006.what: Conditional checkpoint (TA-03 includes HR leave). Configures approval requirements for leave requests through the Approvals module.
APR-DREQ-006.why: Leave approval ensures adequate staffing and prevents scheduling conflicts.
confirm_first: Are leave requests approved by the direct manager?
Are there leave types that require HR approval in addition to manager approval?
Has the leave request notification flow been tested?
common_mistakes: Duplicating leave approval in both HR module and Approvals module.

APR-DREQ-007.title: Document signing approval
APR-DREQ-007.what: Conditional checkpoint (TA-03 includes Document signing). Configures approval requirements for document signing requests.
APR-DREQ-007.why: Document signing approval ensures that documents sent for signature have been reviewed and authorized before committing the organization.
confirm_first: Which document types require pre-signing approval?
Who approves signing requests?
Has the approval-to-signing flow been tested?
common_mistakes: Not linking signing approval to the Sign module workflow.

subscriptions.label: Subscriptions
subscriptions.overview: Governs recurring revenue models, renewal policy, and recurring invoicing. Subscriptions depends on sales configuration for baseline commercial policy. Coverage gap: sale.order.template and sale.order not in governed apply surface.

SUB-FOUND-001.title: Subscription module foundation
SUB-FOUND-001.what: Establishes the subscription module baseline — confirms subscription plans, recurring periods, and renewal settings are available. Depends on SAL-DREQ-001.
SUB-FOUND-001.why: Subscriptions automate recurring revenue. Without foundation, recurring invoices are not generated and renewal management is manual.
confirm_first: Is the Subscriptions module installed?
What are the subscription plan types (monthly, annual)?
Has the recurring billing cycle been defined?
common_mistakes: Starting subscription sales before the billing cycle and renewal policy are configured.

SUB-DREQ-001.title: Subscription plan and pricing configuration
SUB-DREQ-001.what: Configures subscription plans with pricing, recurrence periods, and auto-renewal settings.
SUB-DREQ-001.why: Subscription plans drive the recurring billing engine. Incorrect plan configuration causes wrong invoice amounts or billing frequencies.
confirm_first: What are the subscription pricing tiers?
Are there different plans for different customer segments?
Is auto-renewal enabled and at what terms?
common_mistakes: Not testing the full subscription lifecycle (create, renew, cancel, invoice).

SUB-DREQ-002.title: Subscription renewal and lifecycle policy
SUB-DREQ-002.what: Configures subscription renewal, cancellation, and lifecycle management rules.
SUB-DREQ-002.why: Renewal policy affects revenue recognition and customer retention. Without lifecycle rules, subscriptions may auto-renew or cancel unexpectedly.
confirm_first: What is the renewal notification period?
How are cancellations handled (immediate, end of period)?
Are there early termination penalties?
common_mistakes: Not configuring renewal reminders, causing subscriptions to lapse without customer notification.

SUB-DREQ-003.title: Subscription-accounting journal linkage
SUB-DREQ-003.what: Conditional checkpoint (FC-01=Full accounting). Informational confirmation that subscription invoicing is linked to the correct accounting journals and revenue recognition settings.
SUB-DREQ-003.why: Subscription revenue may require deferred revenue recognition. Without journal linkage, recurring invoices may post to incorrect accounts.
confirm_first: Is deferred revenue recognition needed for subscriptions?
Has the subscription invoice journal been configured?
Has finance reviewed the subscription accounting flow?
common_mistakes: Not configuring deferred revenue accounts for annual subscriptions billed upfront.

SUB-GL-001.title: Subscriptions go-live readiness
SUB-GL-001.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming subscription configuration is finalized for production billing.
SUB-GL-001.why: Subscription billing errors affect recurring revenue and customer relationships. Configuration must be correct before the first billing cycle.
confirm_first: Has a full subscription lifecycle been tested (create, invoice, renew)?
Are all plans and pricing finalized?
Has the subscription team been trained?
common_mistakes: Going live with subscriptions without testing the automatic invoice generation.

rental.label: Rental
rental.overview: Governs rental inventory, reservations, pricing periods, and return handling. Rental depends on master data and inventory for product tracking. Coverage gap: sale.order and product.template not in governed apply surface.

RNT-FOUND-001.title: Rental module foundation
RNT-FOUND-001.what: Establishes rental module baseline — confirms rental products, pricing periods, and availability tracking are available. Depends on MAS-DREQ-001.
RNT-FOUND-001.why: Rental operations require products configured for rental with period-based pricing and availability tracking. Without foundation, rental orders cannot be processed.
confirm_first: Is the Rental module installed?
Are rental products configured with correct product type (storable)?
Has the rental pricing structure been defined (daily, weekly, monthly)?
common_mistakes: Configuring rental products as consumable instead of storable, losing individual asset tracking.

RNT-DREQ-001.title: Rental pricing and order configuration
RNT-DREQ-001.what: Configures rental pricing periods, order workflow, and pickup/return procedures. Depends on SAL-DREQ-001.
RNT-DREQ-001.why: Rental pricing must account for time-based charges. Without configuration, rental orders use standard sales pricing instead of period-based rates.
confirm_first: What are the rental pricing periods (hourly, daily, weekly)?
Are there different pricing tiers for different durations?
Has the pickup and return workflow been defined?
common_mistakes: Not configuring rental-specific pricing, causing all rentals to use the base product price.

RNT-DREQ-002.title: Rental-inventory return flow
RNT-DREQ-002.what: Informational checkpoint confirming rental return flow is configured in inventory. Depends on INV-FOUND-003. Coverage gap: sale.order not in governed apply surface.
RNT-DREQ-002.why: Rental returns must update availability and condition tracking. Without the return flow, returned assets do not become available for re-rental.
confirm_first: Is the rental return operation type configured?
Are condition checks performed on return?
How are damaged returns handled?
common_mistakes: Not creating a return operation type, causing returned rental assets to disappear from availability.

RNT-GL-001.title: Rental go-live readiness
RNT-GL-001.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming rental configuration is finalized for operational rental orders.
RNT-GL-001.why: Rental errors after go-live affect asset utilization, pricing accuracy, and customer experience.
confirm_first: Has a complete rental cycle been tested (reserve, pickup, return, invoice)?
Are all rental products and pricing finalized?
Has the rental team been trained?
common_mistakes: Going live without testing the return-and-reinventory flow.

field_service.label: Field Service
field_service.overview: Governs on-site service execution, scheduling, task completion, and service-related inventory/billing linkage on hr.employee (technician assignment). Coverage gap: project.task not in governed apply surface.

FSV-FOUND-001.title: Field service module foundation
FSV-FOUND-001.what: Establishes the field service baseline — confirms field tasks, technician teams, and dispatch workflow are available. Depends on PRJ-DREQ-001 (project task configuration).
FSV-FOUND-001.why: Field service requires project tasks to be configured for on-site work. Without foundation, field tasks cannot be created or dispatched.
confirm_first: Is the Field Service module installed?
Has the technician team structure been defined?
What types of field service tasks does the business perform?
common_mistakes: Activating field service without configuring the project module first.

FSV-DREQ-001.title: Field service task and scheduling configuration
FSV-DREQ-001.what: Configures field service task types, scheduling rules, and technician assignment on hr.employee.
FSV-DREQ-001.why: Task configuration determines how work is scheduled, dispatched, and tracked. Without it, field tasks are created without proper assignment or scheduling.
confirm_first: What are the field service task categories?
How are technicians assigned to tasks (geographic, skill-based)?
Is the scheduling calendar configured for technician availability?
common_mistakes: Not configuring technician availability, causing scheduling conflicts.

FSV-DREQ-002.title: Field service billing configuration
FSV-DREQ-002.what: Configures field service billing — how on-site work is invoiced to customers. Depends on SAL-DREQ-001.
FSV-DREQ-002.why: Field service billing captures labor, travel, and material costs for customer invoicing. Without configuration, field work is not billed.
confirm_first: Is field service billed on time & materials or fixed price?
Are travel costs billable?
Has the field service invoicing flow been tested?
common_mistakes: Not configuring the service product for field service billing.

FSV-DREQ-003.title: Field service inventory integration
FSV-DREQ-003.what: Conditional checkpoint (OP-01=Yes). Informational confirmation that field service is integrated with inventory for on-site material consumption. Depends on INV-FOUND-001 and MAS-DREQ-001.
FSV-DREQ-003.why: Field technicians consume parts on-site. Without inventory integration, parts consumption is not tracked and stock levels are inaccurate.
confirm_first: Will technicians consume parts from a mobile/truck stock?
Is the field service warehouse/location configured?
Has a test field task with parts consumption been run?
common_mistakes: Not configuring a field service warehouse location, causing all parts to be consumed from the main warehouse.

FSV-GL-001.title: Field service go-live readiness
FSV-GL-001.what: Go-live checkpoint confirming field service configuration is finalized for operational dispatch.
FSV-GL-001.why: Field service errors after go-live directly affect customer service quality and technician productivity.
confirm_first: Has a complete field service cycle been tested (create, dispatch, complete, invoice)?
Are all task types and technician assignments finalized?
Have technicians been trained on the mobile interface?
common_mistakes: Going live without testing the mobile field service experience.
